g.onclick: sendPrompt('What events does the matching engine fire?')
sendPrompt('How do TradeListener and DepthListener callbacks work?')
sendPrompt('What fields go into a depth or trade feed message?')
sendPrompt('How does FAST protocol encoding work?')
sendPrompt('What does the subscriber do when it receives a feed message?')
sendPrompt('Why are sequence numbers critical in market data feeds?')
sendPrompt('How does applying a depth event update the internal book replica?')
sendPrompt('How does strategy logic use the internal book replica?')

svg.text: pre-allocated, no malloc in hot path
Trade message: fires once per match — symbol, qty, cost (price × qty). Each trade is independent, not incremental.
Depth incremental: fires when any price level changes — sends only changed levels (usually just 1–2). Fast, small.
Depth full snapshot: sent to new subscribers or after a gap — all 5 levels both sides. Bootstraps the replica.
